TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: ka sitsuayan izua ti sa Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: ka siʦuajan izua ti sa cukucuku.
S.FORM: ka sicuayan izua ti sa Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: ka siʦuajan izua ti sa ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: Once upon a time there was a woman called Tjukutjuku.

S.FORM: qau mapu tua ḍipupun katua aluvetjuɫatan, sa vaik a sema tjatjan.
S.PHON: qau mapu tua ɖipupun katua aɭuvəcuʎatan, sa vaik a səma cacan.
S.FORM: qau mapu tua dripupun katua aluvetjuljatan, sa vaik a sema tjatjan.
S.PHON: qau mapu tua ɖipupun katua aɣuvəʦuʎatan, sa vaik a səma ʦaʦan.
S.TRANSL: She chewed the nuts of the ḍipupun and aluvetjuɫatan trees, and went to the spring.

S.FORM: qau sidjilay i tjatjan azua inapuan.
S.PHON: qau siɉiɭaj i cacan azua inapuan.
S.FORM: qau sidjilay i tjatjan azua inapuan.
S.PHON: qau siɉiɣaj i ʦaʦan azua inapuan.
S.TRANSL: She spat into the spring what she had chewed;

S.FORM: qau sa tsemuvuq azua djilay tua saviki.
S.PHON: qau sa ʦəmuvuq azua ɉiɭaj tua saviki.
S.FORM: qau sa cemuvuq azua djilay tua saviki.
S.PHON: qau sa ʦəmuvuq azua ɉiɣaj tua saviki.
S.TRANSL: and the spit sprouted into an areca tree.

S.FORM: qau meqatsa azua saviki, venangaɫ.
S.PHON: qau məqaʦa azua saviki, vənaŋaʎ.
S.FORM: qau meqaca azua saviki, venangalj.
S.PHON: qau məqaʦa azua saviki, vənaŋaʎ.
S.TRANSL: The areca tree got bigger and bore fruit;

S.FORM: qau kisavikin anga ni sa Tjukutjuku tu apuin.
S.PHON: qau kisavikin aŋa ni sa cukucuku tu apuin.
S.FORM: qau kisavikin anga ni sa Tjukutjuku tu apuin.
S.PHON: qau kisavikin aŋa ni sa ʦukuʦuku tu apuin.
S.TRANSL: and Tjukutjuku got areca nuts from it to chew.

S.FORM: azua ti sa Tjukutjuku ka vaik a kivala sema zua itjua tapaw nua mareqali, inika pinalimasudj a tapaw.
S.PHON: azua ti sa cukucuku ka vaik a kivaɭa səma zua icua tapaw nua marəqaɭi, inika pinaɭimasuɉ a tapaw.
S.FORM: azua ti sa Tjukutjuku ka vaik a kivala sema zua itjua tapaw nua mareqali, inika pinalimasudj a tapaw.
S.PHON: azua ti sa ʦukuʦuku ka vaik a kivaɣa səma zua iʦua tapaw nua maRəqaɣi, inika pinaɣimasuɉ a tapaw.
S.TRANSL: When Tjukutjuku went to amuse herself to the house of some men friends, the house had not been tidied.

S.FORM: qau suapan ni sa Tjukutjuku; sa tjangtjangan ta saviki, sa vaik a tsemikel a tjumaq.
S.PHON: qau suapan ni sa cukucuku; sa caŋcaŋan ta saviki, sa vaik a ʦəmikəɭ a cumaq.
S.FORM: qau suapan ni sa Tjukutjuku; sa tjangtjangan ta saviki, sa vaik a cemikel a tjumaq.
S.PHON: qau suapan ni sa ʦukuʦuku; sa ʦaŋʦaŋan ta saviki, sa vaik a ʦəmikəɣ a ʦumaq.
S.TRANSL: Tjukutjuku swept the house, and left some crushed and prepared areca nuts, and went back home.

S.FORM: mangtjez a mareqali.
S.PHON: maŋcəz a marəqaɭi.
S.FORM: mangtjez a mareqali.
S.PHON: maŋʦəz a maRəqaɣi.

S.FORM: "ɫaqa, tima nasemuap tua tja tapaw?
S.PHON: "ʎaqa, tima nasəmuap tua ca tapaw
S.FORM: "ljaqa, tima nasemuap tua tja tapaw?
S.PHON: "ʎaqa, tima nasəmuap tua ʦa tapaw
S.TRANSL: “What’s this? Who has swept our house?

S.FORM: tjamanguaq a tja raungen," aya.
S.PHON: camaŋuaq a ca rauŋən," aja.
S.FORM: tjamanguaq a tja raungen," aya.
S.PHON: ʦamaŋuaq a ʦa Rauŋən," aja.
S.TRANSL: It would be better if we laid in wait for them,” they said.

S.FORM: qau raungen nua mareqali.
S.PHON: qau rauŋən nua marəqaɭi.
S.FORM: qau raungen nua mareqali.
S.PHON: qau Rauŋən nua maRəqaɣi.
S.TRANSL: So the men friends lay in wait.

S.FORM: manu mangtjez azua ti sa Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: manu maŋcəz azua ti sa cukucuku.
S.FORM: manu mangtjez azua ti sa Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: manu maŋʦəz azua ti sa ʦukuʦuku.

S.FORM: qau puvaɫavan anga nua mareqali, aya a tjautsikel.
S.PHON: qau puvaʎavan aŋa nua marəqaɭi, aja a cauʦikəɭ.
S.FORM: qau puvaljavan anga nua mareqali, aya a tjaucikel.
S.PHON: qau puvaʎavan aŋa nua maRəqaɣi, aja a ʦauʦikəɣ.
S.TRANSL: and she became the wife of (one of?) the men friends, according to the story.

S.FORM: saka mavan nu sika izua nua saviki.
S.PHON: saka mavan nu sika izua nua saviki.
S.FORM: saka mavan nu sika izua nua saviki.
S.PHON: saka mavan nu sika izua nua saviki.
S.TRANSL: And that’s the reason why there are areca trees now.
